title: Apply Security Context to Your Pods and Containers

input_row.title: Apply Security Context to Your Pods and Containers
input_row.description: Apply Security Context to Your Pods and Containers
input_row.rationale: A security context defines the operating system security settings (uid, gid, capabilities, SELinux role, etc..) applied to a container. When designing your containers and pods, make sure that you configure the security context for your pods, containers, and volumes. A security context is a property defined in the deployment yaml. It controls the security parameters that will be assigned to the pod/container/volume. There are two levels of security context: pod level security context, and container level security context.
input_row.audit: Review the pod definitions in your cluster and verify that you have security contexts defined as appropriate. OpenShift's Security Context Constraint feature is on by default in OpenShift 4 and applied to all pods deployed. SCC selection is determined by a combination of the values in the securityContext and the rolebindings for the account deploying the pod. Run the following command to obtain a list of pods that are using privileged security context constraints: oc get pods -A -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.metadata.annotations."openshift.io/scc"|test("privileged"?)) | .metadata.name' Review each pod to ensure it requires the use of privileged security context constraints, which is the most relaxed security context constraint available in OpenShift by default. Run the following command to obtain a list of pods that are not using security context constraints at all: oc get pods -A -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.metadata.annotations."openshift.io/scc" == null) | .metadata.name' Review each pod and determine if there is an existing security context constraint that it should be using.
input_row.remediation: Follow the Kubernetes documentation and apply security contexts to your pods. For a suggested list of security contexts, you may refer to the CIS Security Benchmark for Docker Containers.
input_row.impact: If you incorrectly apply security contexts, you may have trouble running the pods.
input_row.default_value: By default, no security contexts are automatically applied to pods.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Access the OpenShift Console**: Log in to the IBM Cloud OpenShift console (https://cloud.ibm.com/openshift).
2. **Navigate to the Pods Section**: Go to the "Workloads" menu on the left-hand side and select "Pods" to view all pods deployed in your cluster.
3. **Audit Security Context**: For each pod listed, click on the pod name to view its details and check the YAML configuration. Verify if the `securityContext` section is present.
4. **Review Privileged Security Context**: Execute the command:
   ```shell
   oc get pods -A -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.metadata.annotations."openshift.io/scc"|test("privileged"?) ) | .metadata.name'
   ```
   Look for each pod that is using privileged security context constraints. Review if these pods genuinely need such relaxed permissions by cross-referencing their functionalities.
5. **Identify Pods Without Security Context**: Similarly, execute:
   ```shell
   oc get pods -A -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.metadata.annotations."openshift.io/scc" == null) | .metadata.name'
   ```
   Identify pods that do not have any assigned security context. Review their dependencies and ensure there's a valid security context they should adhere to.
6. **Document Findings**: For each pod checked, document the security contexts applied and any issues found related to inadequate or misconfigured security contexts.
7. **Confirm Compliance**: By cross-referencing your findings with the CIS Security Benchmark for Docker Containers and the Kubernetes security context documentation, determine if your pod deployments meet the required security standards. 

Expected secure configuration would have appropriate `securityContext` sections defined, and privileged security contexts used only when necessary. If any pods do not meet the criteria, corrective actions must be taken to apply appropriate security contexts.
